← Minh Tran

CMSC 19929: Creating Computer Games for Learning

University of Chicago Pre-College Program — Summer 2026

Welcome to Computer Games For Learning!

Please the full course syllabus carefully at least once to make sure you understand the course logistics and expectations!

Classroom:

  • Time: July 7–23, 2026, Monday through Friday (9AM - 3PM)
  • Location: Ryerson Phys Lab (Rm 255 - lectures / Rm 277 - labs).

Asynchronous Communication:

  • All course materials will be released on this website, following the course schedule below.
  • All assignments will be submitted and graded via Gradescopes (link TBA).
  • General course communication will occur on Piazza (link TBA).
    • This is the best way to ask any and all questions related to this course. We will check Piazza at least three times per day and try to address your questions as soon as possible.
    • For any general or clarification questions that don’t leak information about your solution, please ask as a public question.
    • For any questions they may leak your solution or contain sensitive information that you do not wish other students or graders to see, you are free to email me. Please see course syllabus for some notes on sending email.

Course Schedule

Please note that the current courese schedule is tentative and subjected to changes!

Week Day Date Lecture (mornings) Lab (afternoons)
Week 1 Tue 7/7
Part 1: Introduction & What is an Educational Game?
Part 2: Introducing Python, Variables and Statements
Coding practice (indiv.)
Design exercise (group)
Wed 7/8
Part 1: Constructivism & Flow Theory
Part 2: Strings and Lists
Coding practice (indiv.)
Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #0: Finalize group members
Thu 7/9
Part 1: ZPD, Scaffolding, and Learning Trajectories
Part 2: Functions and Conditionals
Coding practice (indiv.)
Design exercise (group)
Fri 7/10
Part 1: Game Elements
Part 2: Iteration
Coding practice (indiv.)
Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #1: Target Users and Learning Content
Week 2 Mon 7/13
Part 1: Classes and Objects
Part 2: Game Elements (cont.) & Game Design Frameworks
Guest Speaker: TBA
Coding practice (indiv.)
Open work time (group)
Tue 7/14
Part 1: Motivation & Assessment
Part 2: Quander
Guest Speaker: TBA
Coding practice (indiv.)
Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #2: Basic Game Design
Wed (Zoom) 7/15
Part 1: Introducing Pygame
Part 2: Inclusive Design
Guest Speaker: Aayushi Dangol (foundry10)
Coding practice (indiv.): PyGame Warmup
Open work time (group)
Thu 7/16 Exploring Pygame
Coding practice (indiv.): Bubble Pop
Open work time (group)
Fri 7/17 (Students) — Flash Talk and Peer Review Workshop Field Trip: UChicago Weston Game Lab
Week 3 Mon 7/20 AI in Design and Education
Coding practice (indiv.): Memory Tiles
Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #3: Final Game Design & MVP Implementation "Contract"
Tue 7/21 AI in Games
Coding practice (indiv.): Maze Navigator
Open work time (group)
Wed 7/22 (Students) — Project Presentation (“Final Exam”) Open work time (group)
Thu 7/23 (Movie Time!) — TBA Open work time (group)
[Extra Credit] Project Milestone #4: MVP Implementation

Milestones

Milestone Description Due
#0 (Non-graded) Finalize group members Wed 7/8
#1 Target Users and Learning Content Fri 7/10
#2 Basic Game Design Tue 7/14
#3 Final Game Design Mon 7/20
#4 (Extra Credit) MVP Implementation Thu 7/23
